question.text: Vikram, a Hindu male, dies intestate leaving behind his widow Shalini, son Arjun, daughter Kavita, mother Savitri, and father Ramesh. Under the Hindu Succession Act 1956, which of the following persons is NOT a Class I heir?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under the Hindu Succession Act, Class I heirs include son, daughter, widow, and mother — but NOT the father. Father is a Class II heir. There are 16 types of Class I heirs in total. **The Trap:** This is one of the most frequently tested traps. Most candidates assume father is a Class I heir alongside mother. Remember: Mother = Class I, Father = Class II. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-B heavily tests Hindu Succession Act provisions. This father vs mother distinction appears repeatedly. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When advising Hindu clients on intestate succession, always clarify that father inherits only if no Class I heir exists. This surprises many families.
